# Subnet 30D τ▼30-day emissionsTotal TAO allocated. Compare WoW to detect validator conviction shifts. Daily τ▼Daily average30D emissions ÷ 30. Current daily allocation rate to this subnet. Share▼Emission share% of 30D emissions this subnet captures. Higher = more validator conviction. Burn τ▼30-day burnHigher burn = stronger economic activity. Low burn + high emissions = subsidy dependency. Coverage▼Burn coverageBurn ÷ Emissions. >100% = deflationary (profitable). <30% = subsidy-dependent. 7d Δ▼7-day changeDeclining WoW = validators losing conviction. Sustained decline → deregistration risk.

EXIT NOW: SN67 — Score 94, 1 validator left, deregisters within days. REDUCE 25%: SN103 (88) — validator dependency >50%, unstake by EOW. SET STOP-LOSS: SN88 (82), SN112 (78) — exit if score exceeds 85 within 7d.
Emissions Decline?
12
Emissions fell >40% in 30d
Emissions Decay>40% decline = validators divesting. Subnet losing its "revenue" allocation. 30% weight in composite score.
Validators Exited?
9
2+ validators unstaked in 14d
Validator Exits2+ unstaked in 14d = vote of no confidence. Below min. threshold = forced deregistration. 25% weight.
Miners Below 25%?
15
Slot fill below 25% threshold
Miner Attrition<25% fill = can't deliver value. Leading indicator — attrition precedes deregistration by ~21d. 20% weight.
Reg. Cost <0.01τ?
8
Zero market demand for slot
Reg. Cost Collapse<0.01τ = zero market demand for this slot. Equivalent to sub-$1 stock (delisting risk). 15% weight.

Critical Risk Subnets — Composite Scoring
# Subnet Emissions 30d▼30-day emissions changeHow much this subnet's daily TAO allocation has declined. >50% decline triggers the emissions decay component of the composite score (30% weight). Validators▼Active / RequiredCurrent validators vs. minimum required. Falling below minimum = automatic deregistration (25% weight). Miners▼Active / Total slotsMiner fill rate. Below 25% = can't deliver value. Leading indicator — attrition precedes exits by ~14d (20% weight). Reg. Cost▼Slot registration costMarket price to acquire this subnet's slot. Below 0.01τ = no demand (15% weight). Score▼Composite risk score0-100 weighted: emissions decay 30%, validators 25%, miner fill 20%, reg. cost 15%, recent exits 10%. ≥85 = deregistration within ~7 days. 65-84 = within ~30 days.

Resources / Methodology

Methodology

Scoring framework, tool definitions, and data sources

Jump To
Subnet RiskDeregistrationMomentumValidatorsTAO FlowRevenue
Subnet Risk Score — Scoring Architecture

0–100 composite score from 13 on-chain metrics across 4 dimensions. All inputs are publicly available — no proprietary data access required. Since Bittensor's 2025 shift to flow-based emissions, allocation efficiency is the primary survival signal, which is why Emissions Economics carries the largest weight.

INVESTIBILITY TIERS
A · 80+ B · 60–79 C · 40–59 D · 20–39 F · <20

Thresholds calibrated so that historically, subnets scoring below 60 had >3× higher deregistration probability within 90 days. Tiers are quality filters, not investment recommendations.

FOUR RISK DIMENSIONS
DimensionWeightFocusMetrics
Emissions Economics40%Emission capture and retention efficiencyEmission share, trend, efficiency, stake retention
Tokenomic Sustainability25%Alpha token liquidity, price stability, miner sell pressureAlpha/TAO ratio, pool depth, sell pressure
Fundamental Sustainability20%Product-market fit and revenue potential beyond emissionsExternal revenue, API usage, customer count, code activity
Strategic Positioning15%Team strength proxy: governance, validator support, ecosystem tiesValidator count, governance score, team visibility
SCORING MECHANICS
Layer 1: Quantitative
13 metrics scored 1–5 (integer), weighted by dimension → 0–100 composite. 5 binary flags overlay: single-validator dependency, closed-source code, no emissions 30d+, anonymous team, pending deregistration.
Layer 2: Qualitative Flags
Displayed alongside the quantitative score — not baked in. Covers team credibility, competitive moat, regulatory exposure, and known data gaps that the model can't capture.
Update Cadence
Full scores updated quarterly. Binary flags and dimension sub-scores refresh with each tool update (daily for most tools).
Deregistration Index
Key Finding

Bottom-10 subnets cluster within a 6% price spread before removal. 42 implied deregistration events since June 2025, median cadence of 5 days. Entering this zone predicts removal within ~14 days.

Tracks which subnets are closest to removal. When all 128 slots are full and a new subnet registers, Bittensor removes the lowest-ranked non-immune subnet.

REMOVAL MECHANISM
StepWhat HappensKey Data
1. Slot FullAll 128 slots occupied. New subnet burns TAO to register.Slot count (128/128)
2. Pruning RankSubnets ranked by pruning score (stake + emissions + performance). Lowest identified.Pruning score, position
3. Immunity CheckIf lowest-ranked is within 7-day immunity window, skip to next-lowest.Registration timestamp
4. RemovalSubnet deregistered. Miners/validators ejected. Staked TAO returned.Deregistration event
Subnet Momentum

Tracks which subnets are gaining or losing emission share — a leading indicator of validator confidence. Post-halving emissions are fixed at 3,600τ/day, so it's a zero-sum game: one subnet's gain is another's loss.

SignalDefinitionImplication
Rising ShareEmission percentage increasing vs. network average.Validators adding weight — bullish.
Falling ShareEmission percentage decreasing. Often precedes stake outflows.Validator confidence eroding. Watch for cascading withdrawals.
Category RotationAggregate shifts at category level (AI/ML, Compute, DeFi, Data).Macro signal: capital rotating between sector themes.
Validator Concentration Index

Measures systemic risk from validator centralisation. A subnet dependent on 1–3 validators is fragile — if a major validator withdraws, the subnet can lose its emission allocation overnight. Uses the Herfindahl-Hirschman Index (HHI): a concentration score from 0 (perfectly distributed) to 10,000 (single validator). Above 2,500 = high risk.

MetricThresholdRisk Level
Top-3 Stake Share>33%SYSTEMIC
HHI Score>2,500HIGH CONCENTRATION
Single-Validator Dependency>50% from 1 validatorBINARY FLAG
Delegation PatternsNet outflow >5% / weekWATCH LIST
TAO Flow Monitor

Tracks TAO flowing into and out of subnet alpha pools over 24–72h windows. When multiple large wallets (500τ+) converge on the same subnet within a short timeframe, it's a "smart money" signal. Data from on-chain pool transactions, labelled wallets (OTF, dao5, Yuma, known funds), and pool depth snapshots at 15-minute intervals.

SIGNAL CLASSIFICATION
SignalDefinitionWhen to Use
ACCELAccelerating inflow — increasing volume over consecutive windows.Strongest conviction signal. Often precedes 7D price moves.
INFLOWSteady positive flow above baseline.Accumulation phase — confirms momentum signals.
STEADYNet flow within ±5% of 7D average.No directional signal. Hold or monitor.
OUTFLOWConsistent withdrawals below baseline.Distribution phase. Cross-check with momentum.
DRAINHeavy exits — >20% pool reduction in 7D.Capitulation signal. Check deregistration proximity.
Revenue Tracker

Measures external revenue — income from real customers, not emission subsidies. Classified as Verified (on-chain confirmed) or Estimated (inferred from API usage / team disclosures). Only 8 of 128 subnets generate meaningful external revenue.

MetricFormulaInterpretation
Revenue / τ Emitted30D external revenue ÷ total τ emissions received.Does the subnet earn enough to justify its allocation?
Emission EfficiencyDaily revenue ÷ (daily τ × TAO spot price).100% = self-sustaining. <20% = pure emission subsidy.
Revenue MultipleAlpha market cap ÷ annualised revenue.Lower = cheaper relative to revenue. Comparable to P/S ratio.
Growth-Adjusted MultipleRevenue Multiple ÷ (QoQ revenue growth%).<0.10 = market underpricing growth. Comparable to PEG.
Data Sources & Reliability
DATA SOURCES
On-chain: Taostats API, subtensor chain queries
Revenue: API monitoring, team disclosures, on-chain tx analysis
Validators: Delegation records, stake snapshots
Alpha pools: dTAO pool depth, alpha/TAO exchange rates
Cadence: Scores quarterly, tools daily
WHAT TO DISTRUST
Deregistration Index: Directional only — 2 observed removals limits precision. Don't use alone for low-conviction calls.
Revenue data: 120 subnets self-report or report nothing. Treat non-verified revenue as estimates.
TAO Flow labels: Wallet labelling is probabilistic. "OTF Wallet" = high confidence; unlabelled addresses = moderate.
Historical depth: Time-series start June 2025. Pre-dTAO data unavailable.
Pruning rank: EMA parameters undocumented by OTF. Our model reverse-engineers from observed outcomes.

Stress Test Lab

Updated Mar 9, 2026
Stress test portfolio under 3 scenarios: OTF exit (47% withdrawal), liquidity drain (20% daily outflow), emissions halt (24h suspension). holdings ($1.024B NAV) and 90-day historical volatility. Three pre-built scenarios below; custom parameters available via methodology panel.
P = 8%
Scenario A
Top-3 Validator Exit
-$184M
dao5, Taostats, and Polychain exit simultaneously. 54 shared subnets lose validation. Emissions redistributed to remaining validators over 7 epochs. Cascading deregistrations in 6 subnets with <50% miner fill post-exit.
P = 15%
Scenario B
TAO Price -50%
-$389M
TAO drops from $412 to $206 over 14 days. Alpha token correlations amplify losses (avg beta 1.4x). Liquid TAO positions ($238M) mark-to-market immediately. Illiquid subnet stakes ($786M) face 21-day unstaking queue.
P = 25%
Scenario C
Deregistration Cascade
-$97M
7 at-risk subnets (Khala Score >75) deregister within 30 days. Emissions from deregistered subnets flow to remaining 45. Portfolio holds 3 of 7 at-risk positions ($97M combined). Forced exit at 12-18% slippage.

Estimated Recovery Timeline: Scenario A

IMPACT
0-48h
TROUGH + STABILIZATION
2-14 days
RECOVERY TO 90% NAV
14-45 days
Day 0: Event triggers Day 2: Peak drawdown -$184M Day 14: Emissions stabilize Day 45: Est. 90% recovery
Methodology
Stress Test Methodology

Scenario Construction: Each scenario models a specific tail-risk event with assigned probability based on 12-month historical frequency of comparable events in crypto markets. Impact is calculated position-by-position using current holdings, 90-day volatility, and estimated slippage curves.

Correlation Matrix: Pearson correlations computed on daily log-returns over 90-day rolling window. Subnet returns derived from alpha token price changes plus emissions accrual. High correlation (>0.7) indicates shared validator dependency or category co-movement.

Backtest Methodology: Retrospective application of current portfolio weights to historical subnet returns. Equal-weight benchmark divides capital evenly across all 15 held positions. No transaction costs modeled. Rebalance frequency: weekly. Alpha = Active return minus equal-weight return.

Recovery Estimates: Based on mean reversion analysis of 8 prior validator exit events and 12 prior deregistration events on Bittensor mainnet. 90% recovery threshold assumes no additional shocks during recovery window.

Limitations: Data refresh: Daily 00:00 UTC. On-chain latency: ~8 hours. All figures illustrative pending production integration.

STRESS TEST ADVISORY: SN1-SN3 correlation at 0.82 means your two largest positions ($93.9M combined) effectively behave as one bet. If top-3 validators exit, portfolio loses 18% within 48 hours. Recommended action: reduce SN1 or SN3 exposure by $20M and redistribute to low-correlation subnets (SN13, SN5).

Backtest Signal Card
Validation Passed

Deregistration Risk Score v2 demonstrated 82% accuracy in 9-month backtest. Grades A & B show 100% survival. Grade D shows 62% deregistration within 90 days. Model is production-ready with confidence interval 75–89%.

Alert Rules & Monitoring

Rules Table + Escalation Sidebar

Alert Rules Configuration

Each rule checks hourly. Channels determine where notifications route. Click toggles to enable/disable.
Rule Threshold Channels Severity Status
Khala Score Breach Score > 85 EmailSlackSMS CRITICAL
Position Correlation Pair corr > 0.75 EmailSlack WARNING
Validator Fee Change Fee delta > 2pp Slack WARNING
Miner Fill Drop Fill < 50% EmailSlack CRITICAL
Portfolio Drawdown NAV drop > 5% (7d) EmailSMS CRITICAL
Allocation Drift Drift > 3pp from target Slack INFO
New Subnet Launch Any new registration EmailWebhook INFO
Validator Downtime Uptime < 95% (7d) EmailSlack WARNING
Emissions Share Drop Held subnet share < 1% Slack WARNING
TAO Price Move 24h change > 10% SMSSlack WARNING
Weekly Health Digest Every Sunday 00:00 Email INFO
Concentration Breach HHI > 1000 EmailSlack CRITICAL

Escalation Policy

P0: Immediate 0 MIN
Triggers: Khala Score > 85, NAV drop > 5%, Miner fill < 50%, HHI > 1000
Channels: SMS + Email + Slack simultaneously
Escalation: If unacknowledged after 15 min, calls backup analyst. After 30 min, auto-generates portfolio freeze recommendation.
P1: Urgent 15 MIN
Triggers: Correlation breach, Validator fee change, Emissions drop, Validator downtime, TAO price move
Channels: Email + Slack
Escalation: If unacknowledged after 2h, promotes to P0. Adds to daily briefing if acknowledged but unresolved after 24h.
P2: Informational BATCH
Triggers: Allocation drift, New subnet launch, Weekly digest
Channels: Email (batched daily) or scheduled
Escalation: None. Informational alerts are logged but never escalated. Included in weekly health digest for review.

Current state: 1 pending critical (SN47 Ridges risk score). Average alert-to-acknowledgment time: 22 minutes.

New EntrantsNet +1 this month. New subnets compete for fixed emissions pie, diluting incumbents.
ACCUMULATE: Vanta (SN8) and Targon (SN4) both show sustained 90D growth with verified revenue. Strong validator support, healthy miner fill. EXIT CANDIDATES: Ridges (SN62) now in 4th consecutive week of decline, -34% over 90D. Chutes (SN64) top emitter but only 9% burn coverage, pure subsidy play. VERIFY: HashiChain +80% WoW. Could be organic adoption or validator manipulation. Wait for 2nd week confirmation before sizing.
# Subnet Category Health 30D Emissions▼30-day emissionsTotal TAO allocated. Primary measure of validator conviction in this subnet. 7D Trend7-day sparklineDaily emissions over last 7 days. Visual trend detection. Rising = accumulating conviction. 7D Change▼Week-over-week changeMost recent weekly change. 3+ consecutive declines = declining health grade. 30D Change▼30-day changeMonthly trend. Compare to category peers for relative strength. 90D Change▼90-day changeQuarterly trend. Sustained 90D growth = strong allocation signal. Coverage▼Burn coverageBurn / Emissions. >100% = profitable. <30% = subsidy-dependent. Miners▼Miner fill rateActive miners / total slots. Below 25% = attrition risk. Peer RankCategory peer rankRank within category by 30D emissions. 1 = category leader.

CONCENTRATED 3 of 247 validators rated institutional-grade. OTF (24% stake) is single point of failure. Only 5 validators have 99.9%+ uptime over 90D.

Validator Due Diligence

247 Validators Tracked - 90D Performance Window - Updated Mar 9, 2026Illustrative
ALLOCATE TO: dao5 (DD score 91, 99.97% uptime, 5% fee, 54 subnets) and Taostats (DD 87, 99.95% uptime, 8% fee, independent). AVOID: OTF for new allocations (24% already, concentration ceiling). DIVERSIFY FROM: RT21 (DD 72, declining stake -0.8% 30D, GTV-linked entity risk).
247
Active Validators
+12 from 90D ago
Active ValidatorsValidators with >0 stake and active in at least 1 subnet. Growth = decentralization trend.
3
Institutional Grade
DD Score 85+
Institutional GradeMeets all criteria: 99.9%+ uptime, transparent fees, known entity, diversified subnet coverage, no slashing history.
99.82%
Avg Top-10 Uptime
90D window
Average UptimeWeighted by stake. Target: >99.9%. Below 99.5% = operational risk for delegators.
7.4%
Avg Fee Rate
Range: 0-18%
Average FeeCommission charged on emissions. 0% = unsustainable (future fee hike risk). >15% = expensive.
0
Slashing Events 90D
Network-wide
SlashingPenalty events for validator misbehavior. 0 is excellent. Any slashing = immediate DD review.
RECOMMENDED: For $50M new staking allocation, split: dao5 ($20M, DD 91), Taostats ($15M, DD 87), Polychain ($15M, DD 82). Achieves max diversity benefit: 0 overlap with existing OTF-heavy positions, covers 119 unique subnets (vs 87 with OTF alone). AVOID: Datura (DD 64, declining -2.4%, entity unknown). FirstTensor (DD 71, 0% fee unsustainable, expect fee hike within 6 months).
# Validator Trust Tier DD Score▼Due Diligence Score0-100 composite: Uptime 25%, Fee transparency 20%, Entity verification 20%, Subnet diversity 15%, Track record 10%, Slashing history 10%. Uptime 90D90-day uptimeBlock production reliability. <99.5% = operational concern. Bars show weekly breakdown (green/amber/red). Fee▼Commission rate% of emissions taken as fee. 0% = unsustainable. 5-10% = normal. >15% = expensive. Subnets▼Subnet coverageActive validator in N subnets. Higher = more diversified but potentially overloaded. Stake (Kτ)▼Total delegated stakeCapital entrusted by delegators. Proxy for market trust. 30D▼30-day stake changeGrowing = delegators adding trust. Declining = capital flight. Watch for >5% swings.

DD Score Methodology (0-100)
Uptime (90D) 25%
Fee Transparency 20%
Entity Verification 20%
Subnet Diversity 15%
Track Record 10%
Slashing History 10%
85+ = Institutional. 70-84 = Established. 55-69 = Emerging. <55 = Caution.
Staking Recommendation ($50M)
dao5 ALLOCATE $20M
Highest DD score (91). Known entity (fmr. Owl Ventures). 5% fee is competitive. 54 subnets = strong diversification. Low OTF overlap (42/54 shared).
Uptime: 99.97%Fee: 5%Stake trend: +1.4%
Taostats ALLOCATE $15M
Independent operator (Corcel). 8% fee reasonable. 41 subnets with lowest OTF overlap (28/41). Best diversification benefit for OTF-heavy portfolios.
Uptime: 99.95%Fee: 8%Stake trend: -1.1%
Polychain ALLOCATE $15M
Tier-1 crypto fund. DD 82. Conservative subnet coverage (29) reduces overload risk. Low fee (5%).
Uptime: 99.91%Fee: 5%Stake trend: +0.3%
Datura AVOID
Unknown entity. 18% fee is highest in top 10. Declining stake (-2.4%). One downtime event in 90D. Entity verification: failed.
Entity Correlation Warning
Yuma + Foundry = DCG 11% combined
OTF (protocol team) 24% single entity
RT21 (GTV-linked) 14% entity risk
Treat correlated entities as single exposure when calculating concentration limits. DCG entities (Yuma + Foundry) should count as 11%, not 7% + 4% separately.

YIELD SUSTAINABILITY ALERT

Network emissions trending -2.1% MoM. At current trajectory, fund staking yield drops from 14.2% → 11.1% in 90 days. Revenue impact: -$31.7M annualized. Consider shifting 15% allocation from pure staking to alpha-generating subnets with independent fee revenue.

Ecosystem News (7 Days)
GOV
BIT-47: Proposal to reduce subnet registration cost from 1τ to 0.1τ
Would lower barrier for new subnets. Risk: dilution of emissions across more subnets. Impact: moderate negative for existing holdings.
2h ago
ALERT
OTF announces infrastructure migration to multi-cloud (AWS + GCP)
Positive for validator redundancy. Reduces single-cloud dependency risk. Our OTF concentration still at 24% (above limit).
1d ago
ALPHA
SN1 Apex announces enterprise API partnership with major cloud provider
Revenue catalyst — could add $40K+/mo to subnet revenue. Validates AI/ML compute thesis. Position: $180M (18% NAV).
2d ago
UPDATE
Bittensor v7.4.0 release: improved emission distribution algorithm
More granular emissions weighting by subnet utility. May benefit high-utility subnets (SN1, SN8, SN9) at expense of low-utility.
3d ago
ALERT
SEC issues new guidance on staking-as-a-service — potential regulatory headwind
US-based validators may face registration requirements. OTF (US-based) could be affected. Monitor closely.
5d ago

Position LimitiHard cap at 3% of portfolio per subnet during beta phase Active
Rebalance CadenceiFrequency driven by volatility regime and deregistration risk. Weekly = active monitoring required.iRecommended rebalance frequency based on volatility regime Bi-weekly
Qualitative & Calibration
Revenue AnalysisiAnalyst assessment of revenue quality and sustainability 72
Backtest AccuracyiHistorical backtest accuracy of scoring model for this subnet 81.4%
Data ContingencyiFallback data sources if primary feed goes offline Active
